name: omero-integration
tagline: Python API skill for connecting to OMERO microscopy servers to fetch images, manage ROIs/annotations, run batch analysis and scripts.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped integration skill. It legitimately needs OMERO_HOST/USER/PASSWORD to log into the user's own server (declared up front in frontmatter, no exfiltration seen). No shell-piped code, obfuscation, or hidden instructions. Weak spots: no 'when NOT to use' guidance and no idempotency notes for repeated write/delete operations (delete capability is

highlights.summary: Section 8 'Advanced Features' names delete operations ('Delete objects with callbacks', line 117) as a capability, but the actual delete code/confirmation pattern lives in references/advanced.md, whic
No 'when NOT to use' guidance is present anywhere in the file — a user can't easily tell when this skill is the wrong tool (e.g. non-OMERO imaging platforms).
No explicit statement of what artifact/output the skill produces (e.g. files, tables, images) beyond scattered mentions in 'Common scenarios' bullets; output contract is implicit only.
